hotspots.title: Jordan Bardella’s fuel tax and EU flag standoff
hotspots.blurb: France’s far-right RN leader Jordan Bardella has dominated coverage with his stance on fuel taxes and EU symbols. He supports taxing oil giants' superprofits amid rising fuel prices but opposes sending French troops to the Strait of Hormuz without a ceasefire. Bardella also vowed to remove EU flags from the Élysée Palace if elected president, sparking intense debate.
hotspots.affect: This commands attention due to Bardella’s rising influence in French politics and the polarizing nature of his positions on economic policy and national sovereignty.

hotspots.title: Magyar’s EU funds deal and Sovereignty Office abolition
hotspots.blurb: Hungary’s incoming PM Péter Magyar is negotiating with the EU to unlock billions in frozen funds, a critical issue for Hungary’s economy. He has also pledged to abolish the controversial Sovereignty Protection Office, a move seen as a significant shift from Orbán’s policies. These developments have sparked intense debate about Hungary’s future direction and its relationship with the EU.
hotspots.affect: This story is charged due to the high stakes of EU-Hungary relations and the symbolic break from Orbán’s authoritarian legacy.

hotspots.title: Trump’s WHCD evacuation after shooting
hotspots.blurb: A suspected gunman disrupted the White House Correspondents' Dinner, leading to President Trump’s evacuation. The incident has heightened security concerns and sparked debates about political violence and media safety. Trump’s subsequent remarks and the investigation into the shooter have kept this story in the spotlight.
hotspots.affect: The emotional charge comes from the direct threat to high-profile figures, the broader implications for political discourse, and the sensational nature of the event.

hotspots.title: PP-Vox ‘national priority’ pacts in Spain
hotspots.blurb: Spain’s conservative PP and far-right Vox have formed regional government pacts under the banner of ‘national priority,’ sparking controversy. These agreements include policies that prioritize Spaniards in healthcare, housing, and social services, often at the expense of immigrants. The pacts have drawn criticism from opposition parties and human rights groups.
hotspots.affect: The affective investment stems from the contentious nature of these policies, which touch on immigration, national identity, and social welfare, making them highly polarizing.

hotspots.title: AfD’s rise and democratic backlash in Germany
hotspots.blurb: Germany’s far-right AfD continues to gain traction in polls, sparking a democratic backlash. The party’s controversial stances on immigration, EU policies, and historical narratives have led to protests, legal challenges, and debates about its influence on German politics. The AfD’s rise has also prompted discussions about the limits of democratic tolerance.
hotspots.affect: This story is charged due to the AfD’s extremist rhetoric, its impact on Germany’s political landscape, and the broader implications for European democracy.
